text.title: Compliance-Gate Annotation Output Contract
text.summary: The output is a compliance-gate annotation block attached to (or referenced from) the feature's requirements. A feature without this block does not pass validation.

list.item: feature_id — link to the requirement / user story.
classification: data_types, jurisdiction[], system_role, payments_involved (per r1).
regulations: list of applicable regulations selected from matrix.
must_clauses: array of {clause_id, citation, status: met | not_met | n/a, evidence: req_id or note}.
may_clauses: aggregate count + list of unmet ones with rationale.
open_actions: per not-met MUST clause, a tracked action item.
GDPR / UK-GDPR — when personal data of EU/UK users.
HIPAA — when health data of US persons.
PSD2 / SCA — when EU payment initiation.
SOC 2 — when service-org claims SOC2 attestation.
State-specific (CCPA/CPRA, NYDFS, etc.) — when explicitly in scope.

antipattern.reason: Violates r1. The downstream clause lookup cannot run; defaulting to broad matrices wastes review cycles.

antipattern.description: MUST clause marked N/A without a written reason citation.
antipattern.reason: N/A is the most-abused status. A clause is N/A because of a specific scope decision; that reason must be auditable.

antipattern.description: Sign-off line filled by AI agent or template default.
antipattern.reason: Violates r5. The annotation has no legal weight.

antipattern.description: Jurisdiction = "global" or "worldwide" without a named regulation.
antipattern.reason: Violates r4. "Global" is not jurisdictionally meaningful; pick the most restrictive applicable regulations explicitly.

list.item: Every MUST clause appears with status + evidence.
Every not-met MUST has an open action item with owner.
Sign-off line has a named human and ISO date.
